qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded zlib decompression and protobuf parsing enables denial of service via resource exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same advisory root cause and vulnerable code path: unfurl/parsers/parse_compressed.py calls zlib.decompress() on attacker-controlled compressed data without a maximum decompressed output size, enabling decompression-bomb denial of service.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via dynamic domain construction in shortlink expansion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding describes unvalidated outbound HTTP requests in parse_shortlink.py. The advisory describes unbounded zlib decompression in parse_compressed.py, so the vulnerable function, sink, and root-cause mechanism are different.

selected_finding.title: Unbounded zlib decompression and protobuf parsing enables denial of service via resource exhaustion

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected qualifying finding matches GHSA-h5qv-qjv4-pc5m because it identifies the advisory-described unbounded zlib.decompress() call in unfurl/parsers/parse_compressed.py and the decompression-bomb denial-of-service root cause. The copied PoC was executed under .judge, but Docker failed to resolve the python:3.11-slim base image before the exploit trigger or vulnerable parser logic ran, so this judge attempt provides no runtime activation or exploit-success evidence.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-h5qv-qjv4-pc5m describes unfurl/parsers/parse_compressed.py calling zlib.decompress(decoded) without a maximum output size.
dos_unbounded_decompression/vulnerability_report.json lists parse_compressed.py:43 and parse_compressed.py:82 zlib.decompress() calls without size, ratio, or output limits.

.judge/poc_runs/dos_unbounded_decompression/run.log shows the copied PoC fetched commit 442c2be70885049c57b807220e848988732644cb, then failed while Docker resolved docker.io/library/python:3.11-slim with EOF.
ssrf_shortlink_expansion is a qualifying finding, but it targets parse_shortlink.py SSRF behavior rather than parse_compressed.py decompression.
blockers_to_higher_tier: Docker base image resolution failed before the copied PoC could run exploit_trigger.py or verify.sh.
A retry to fetch the pinned source for a host-side run failed with early EOF, leaving no judge-side runtime artifact that activates the advisory vulnerable logic.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC fetched the pinned vulnerable Unfurl commit, then failed building the Docker image because Docker could not resolve docker.io/library/python:3.11-slim. The vulnerable decompression logic was not reached.
